replies.author: @somethingintheair5373
replies.text:
@Anton-t3j7b And that’s the mistake people make. Socialism was NEVER about caring for the working class - that was merely it’s mask. It’s big government, high taxation, and centralised control. And there’s not one socialist government anywhere in the world that’s been successful in “looking after the working classes.” It always leads to failed economies. Millions have died because of it.

Mrs Thatcher once famously said “The problem with socialism is that eventually you run out of other people's money." 

She also said: “The signposts of socialism point downhill to less freedom, less prosperity, downhill to more muddle, more failure. If we follow them to their destination, they will lead this nation into bankruptcy."

And she was correct.

replies.text:
@Hansiwansi They gave their voters EXACTLY what they promised to them. 

Trump appeasement, 
fewer rights for workers, 
less safety and rights for consumers,
fewer benefits for the average,
lower taxes and less responsibility for the rich, 
longer working hours for everyone.

If you had even the slightest bit of knowledge about politics, you would know that Merz personally advocated drastically curtailing workers' rights even 30 years ago. 
To play surprised now would be nothing less than an admission of one's own boundless incompetence and stupidity. 
Could it be that you have no idea what right-wing politics actually stands for? Whether it's England, the USA, or Germany: the right wing in theese countries IS the greediest financial elite, not freedom of the elite, young fella. ^^

And none of them have the slightest real interest in the delusion of foreign infiltration and people who want to turn your children gay. Planting these fantasies in your head had only one purpose: to gain power.

replies.author: @moru3236
replies.text:
​@Hansiwansi I have absolutely no idea what YOUR education about left-wing politics is, if you think the politics i have listed and that define todays CDU where even close to "left".

Trying to exploit immigrants to inflate the supply of labor and thereby drive down wages IS conservative/right-wing politics. Do you know anything at all? Have you been listening? Don't you understand what real conservative/right-wing politics is and what is just propaganda? 


Your first would be okay if...


Theoretically, as far as I'm concerned, you can do pretty much anything morally, to yourself. 
Take drugs, fight with a kangaroo, stick hooks in your back, tie bungee cords together and jump off a skyscraper with them. 
I don't care about any of that. 

It would be okay if... you didn't harm any innocent people in the process.

In the case of the AfD, you are arbitrarily harming many innocent people with whom I have nothing to do, and you are also harming me as an employee dependent on Germany's international competitiveness and tradeability, AND, of course, my two children, their future, and the future of their children.

Depending on whether someone like Höcke and Gauland or someone a little more moderate prevails, you could end up arbitrarily, physically harming German citizens who have neither a migrant background nor are employees in internationally working companies, who are simply not prepared to shout "Hail xyz" or even dare to demonstrate against their politics. We are currently seeing how quickly this can happen in the USA. 

OF COURSE, I therefore reject you both morally AND opportunistically. 



The second is just an empty claim for now. 

Were you involved in local left-wing politics?
Were you involved in federal left-wing politics?
Were you part of a group of pubescent 15-year-olds who set fire to trash cans and thought they were left-wing?

The claim is so meaningless at first glance, based solely on "trust me bro", wich is, esp. with your combined ignorance towards the nature of todays 'right-wing' politics, not sufficient for me to buy your claim of relevant experience with left-wing politics.

author: @JacK-vk8iu
text:
I'm English. The Amelia character exemplifies how out of touch the UK establishment are with the actual English, Welsh, Scottish and NI inhabitants. They believed their own woke and DEI BS to the point that they actually put the pathways game out.  Animal Farm, and 1984 were warnings against exactly this type of 'rule' ,  

Things have been getting progressively worse since Blair, and that has been accelerating year on year,  we have a Uniparty setup,  as in the parties have all been 'penetrated' by the WEF and globalists and have not honoured manifesto promises for a few decades now, the reason Liz Truss lasted a month is that she was not one of them. 

The Uniparty wants close alignment to the EU and globalists and actively works against the people. It's not a good place to live in that regard. Reform looked initially to be a hope,  but Farage has destroyed that hope for me, he's an egotist, and has opened the doors of Reform to MP's from Tories and Labour, and they do not have good track records and are unlikely to do anything except perpetuate the problem. 

Starmer was responsible in the CPS across the time the grooming gangs were being covered up, responsible for Jimmy Saville not being prosecuted before he died, has a hushed up Ukrainen rent boy scandel pending in the courts and is still in a position to keep harming the countries interests and its people.

 A few decades ago, any one of those scandels and a politician would have resigned, I guess his WEF masters have told him he must stay, he is hated in the country. Starmer has a lower approval rating than Maduro, any chance you can send Trump's boys over to invite him to a cell over there? Pretty sure we (UK) could cover the cost with a 'Go Fund Me', UK's been very grateful to the US for taking Harry and Megan, would you consider Starmer for a cell rather than a mansion?

It does look like there is hope on the political side though, with Ben Habib and Advance UK, and with Rupert Lowe,  both are politicians with some integrity.  The system is rotten in the UK and in serious need of overhaul, Advance UK might have a shot at doing it, Reform UK does not IMO. (Sorry, rant over) You might see why Amelia is so popular and understand?

replies.author: @somethingintheair5373
replies.text:
Little tip here if you wish to write so much:

Add line spaces, ie make short paragraphs.

That way people will much more easily be able to read a long post.

replies.author: @JacK-vk8iu
replies.text: @somethingintheair5373 ☺Ah, sorry, I tend to ramble on sometimes.  I will go through and add some now, although I doubt many will read it anyway, it was a cathartic couple of minutes on the keyboard.  I guess thanks for the feedback. Have a good one.

replies.author: @somethingintheair5373
replies.text: @JacK-vk8iu It’s just I tend to give up half way through long text even if it’s interesting. You’re much more likely to reach people if you give them space (line space - white space) to breath.

replies.author: @JacK-vk8iu
replies.text: @somethingintheair5373 👍Thanks for the note, I broke it up a bit, hopefully that will make it easier for you and others. I guess I'm used to huge chunks of text, and like I said it was more of a cathartic rant. You do have a point though,  there is little point in engaging in a communication if the format (or lack of it) drives others away. Well, sometimes it can be fun to scream at a wall, but that was not my intention in this case. 😊 (although white spaces imply I breathe when on a rant so probably not authenticate in that sense🙄)

author: @gryffensview
text:
Amelia, (name): the Rose of Albion for the new millenium.
Culture, to answer your question, no, the people behind "Pathways" did not want Amelia to be considered to be any form of virture.  "Pathways" is an unabashed indoctrination tool targeting children. Yes, it was taken down, and yes, it was put back up, but people have been commenting that the game has been changed.  Now the game will "help educate Charlie" (the player's avatar) before things go too far. So no, it was never the intent to make Amelia look like any sort of ideal. That's how corrupt these "activist" cultists are.  They have worshipped their twisted dogma so much that it has taken over their own minds.  That's a danger about lying.  People who keep lying in time can not tell the truth, or even know what the truth is themselves.  

Consider these words written two thousand years ago -- that their minds were so corrupt, they change the truth into a lie, and changed the incorruptable things into corruption. Professing themselves wise, they became fools, and gave themselves over to a reprobate mind, to do those things which are not convenient or logical.

Yes, yes. "Preachy." Maybe we need some more preaching. In fact, also from the same text, it describes people who have chosen to embrace a corrupt, self-worshipping ideology. That they are, as quoted: they are filled with all unrighteousness, fornication, wickedness, covetousness, maliciousness; full of envy, murder, debate, deceit, malignity; whisperers, backbiters, haters of virtue, despiteful, proud, boasters, inventors of evil things, disobedient to parents, without understanding, covenantbreakers, without natural affection, implacable, and unmerciful.

Let's be honest. That description is the standard "woke activist."  These are the people who commanded the creation of "Pathways," assembled "Pathways," and demand all of us "obey the education" whether we like it or not. And there in lies the irony.  In their depraved minds, people like Amelia are "monsters and villains.'  They can not show mercy. Since anyone who thinks like Amelia has no other option, what else _can_ we do but rise up and make a stand. 

Here's to Amelia, the Rose of Albion for this new millenium.
